review.summary: The parameter is wired through QAPI/HMP/QMP correctly, but the RDMA chunk size now affects on-the-wire chunk indexing without any peer negotiation or mismatch check. That creates a high-risk interoperability bug if source and destination are configured differently. There is also a secondary validation gap for property-based startup configuration.

findings.title: RDMA chunk size becomes protocol-significant without synchronization between peers
findings.description: The new parameter changes how chunk indices are computed on the source and how they are decoded on the destination, but the patch does not negotiate or transmit the selected chunk size. `ram_chunk_index()` now divides by `migrate_rdma_chunk_size()`, while `rdma_registration_handle()` reconstructs the host address with the destination's local `migrate_rdma_chunk_size()`. If only one side changes `x-rdma-chunk-size`, the destination interprets the incoming chunk number using a different unit, which can point at the wrong address and break or corrupt RDMA migration.
findings.suggestion: Make the chunk size part of the RDMA migration protocol or handshake and reject mismatches explicitly before migration starts. At minimum, ensure the destination decodes registration keys using the source-selected chunk size rather than its local default/configuration.
findings.confidence_reason: The changed code directly uses the local runtime parameter in both chunk encoding and decoding paths, and no synchronization mechanism appears anywhere in the patch.

findings.title: Invalid startup property values can bypass validation and reach a runtime assert
findings.description: The new setting is exposed as a migration property via `DEFINE_PROP_UINT64`, but the range/power-of-two checks were only added to `migrate_params_check()`, which covers QMP/HMP parameter updates. A user can still set `migration.x-rdma-chunk-size` from startup configuration/property paths, and `migrate_rdma_chunk_size()` then enforces validity with `assert(...)`. That turns bad configuration into an abort instead of a normal user-visible error.
findings.suggestion: Validate the property on the startup/property path as well, or move the check into migration setup with a proper error return. Avoid relying on `assert()` for user-provided configuration.
findings.confidence_reason: The patch clearly adds validation only in the parameter update path and uses an assert in the getter; the exact startup path behavior depends on surrounding migration property handling not included here.

disclaimer: LLM-generated draft. Not an authoritative review.
